text.title: MCP Client Integration — Agents, Claude Code, Host Configuration — Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of `mcp-client-integration` must enforce. Verifiable at review time; failures gate the artifact.

rule.statement: Maintain an allow-list of MCP servers; block all others.
rule.rationale: Auto-loading arbitrary servers is the lookalike-tool attack vector.

rule.statement: First call to a new MCP tool surfaces explicit user consent; cache decision per-session.
rule.rationale: Implicit-consent UX causes consent fatigue and unwanted tool execution.

rule.statement: Stdio servers run in subprocess isolation (no shared FS / env); pass only declared env vars.
rule.rationale: Shared env / FS leaks host secrets to MCP servers.

rule.statement: Bridge schema between MCP and SDK is frozen per session; never accept tool-schema deltas mid-session.
rule.rationale: Mid-session tool changes are a tool-permission-escalation vector.

rule.statement: Tool descriptions from MCP servers render in a distinguishable UI surface; never inlined into system prompt.
rule.rationale: Treat tool descriptions as untrusted prose (r5 of mcp-architecture).

rule.statement: Every MCP tool call logged with server_id + tool_name + caller_session + timestamp.
rule.rationale: Without an audit log, post-incident attribution is impossible.
